file_insights.purpose: ViewModel for MainActivity with Hilt dependency injection
key_features: Three public methods for data access

patterns_discovered.description: Dependencies injected via constructor parameters with @Inject annotation
Simple getter methods (getAppName, getPackageName, getGreetingMessage) exposing injected data
benefits: Single point of access for UI layer

patterns_discovered.description: Class and method level KDoc comments explaining purpose and usage

gotchas_discovered.description: Injected dependencies are private and cannot be accessed from outside
Uses string interpolation rather than resource strings
gotchas_discovered.note: May impact localization if multi-language support is needed

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: No error handling for null dependencies
gotchas_discovered.description: Constructor assumes injected dependencies are non-null
gotchas_discovered.note: Acceptable if Hilt guarantees injection; consider @Nullable if optional

key_achievements: Properly integrated with Android Architecture Components (ViewModel)
Followed Kotlin and Android best practices
Created a demonstration of working DI setup

recommendations.suggestion: Replace hardcoded greeting string with string resources from strings.xml
Create unit tests for MainViewModel using Hilt testing utilities and mock dependencies
Consider adding @Nullable annotations if dependencies could potentially be null
Create MainActivity implementation that instantiates MainViewModel using viewModels() delegate
Add usage example in class KDoc showing how MainActivity should use this ViewModel
